mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust demand without inflationary pressure, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series models, or RSAFS growth paired with CPIAUCSL stability yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term positive market repricing over the next week during recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or produce negative returns during expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 210, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches 210, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment indicates a healthy labor market expansion that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative forward returns in >65% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which forecasts negative equity returns over the next quarter during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of late-cycle periods, or the signal shows no predictive power outside of recessionary environments.
